column4: Tallerico et al. Section IV-A direct motorette process statement; Parker LORD CoolTherm EP-2000 identified by reference 19
column5: The in-house motorettes used the same epoxy as the twisted pairs and were cured through the 180 degC post-cure step. The manufacturer-recommended final 210 degC step was intentionally not completed because of the nylon end-board temperature limit. The paper does not report epoxy lot, mix ratio, complete preceding cure schedule, hold durations, ramp rates, vacuum or pressure values for the motorettes, degree of cure, void fraction, thickness distribution, dielectric or thermal bulk-property measurements, uncertainty, or confidence metadata. The two temperatures describe one deliberately incomplete source process state
